questions.q: Match correctly: which decisions are made at the Campaign level?
options: Audience, placements, conversion location
Creative, destination, tracking
Buying type, objective, budget, bid
Pixel installation and CAPI setup
questions.explanation: At the Campaign level: buying type, objective, budget, bid, special ad category. Audience/placements go in the ad set. Creative/destination go in the ad.

questions.q: What is the key difference between Advantage+ campaigns and Advantage+ tools (single-step)?
options: Advantage+ campaigns are free, Advantage+ tools are paid
Advantage+ campaigns automate the ENTIRE campaign (Sales/App/Leads); Advantage+ tools automate ONE aspect (audience, placements, budget, creative, destination)
Advantage+ tools are only available for sales; Advantage+ campaigns for every objective
There is no difference, they are synonyms
questions.explanation: Advantage+ campaigns = complete campaign solutions (they automate everything). Single-step = one specific aspect. The exam sets traps by mixing them.

questions.q: A user has full control of ONE ad account but only partial access to the portfolio. Is this possible?
options: No, permissions are always uniform across the entire portfolio
Yes, fine-grained per-account permissions are possible without having full control of the entire portfolio
Only if the user is a Partner, not if they are People
Only system users can have this type of setup
questions.explanation: A user can have full control of one account WITHOUT having full control of the portfolio. This allows fine-grained permissions while keeping oversight at the portfolio level.

questions.q: Select the 4 types of controls in the Brand Safety & Suitability Center.
options: Inventory filters, Publisher block lists, Topic exclusions, Content type exclusions
Age controls, Geography controls, Device controls, Time controls
Bid caps, Budget caps, Frequency caps, Reach caps
Conversion controls, Engagement controls, Impression controls, Click controls
questions.explanation: The 4 controls of the Brand Safety Center: Inventory filters (Expanded/Moderate/Limited), Publisher block lists, Topic exclusions (Gaming/News/Politics/Religion), Content type exclusions (live video, non-partner).

options: Predicts how much the next campaign will sell
Shows how many experimentally proven recommendations you haven't applied, and updates as you apply them or change the account
Rates the quality of the creative compared to other advertisers
Is a score that GUARANTEES your performance if you reach 100
questions.explanation: A 0-100 score based on recommendations not yet applied. It does NOT predict or guarantee real performance. It updates as you apply recommendations or modify the account.
